zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：短线偏弱震荡运行
zh.content:
AVAX/USD当前维持低位窄幅整理，价格运行在20日均线下方，显示短线空头仍占主导。24小时波动极小，市场观望情绪明显增强，资金活跃度不足，整体趋势偏弱震荡。

从技术指标看，MACD维持负值区域，说明空头动能尚未完全释放结束，短线仍存在进一步回踩风险。SMA20位于8.96附近，高于现价，表明均线形成上方压制，反弹力度暂时有限。

关键支撑位可关注8.80附近，若跌破可能进一步测试8.65区域；上方短线阻力位在8.96至9.05区间，只有重新站稳20日均线，市场情绪才可能逐步转强，形成反弹结构。

操作上建议以谨慎观望或短线高抛低吸为主，激进交易者可等待8.80附近企稳后的反弹机会。若放量突破9.05，可考虑顺势跟进多单，否则仍应控制仓位并注意止损风险。

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
AVAX/USD is trading at 8.8255, remaining under pressure after a modest 24-hour decline of 0.05%. The price is currently below the 20-period simple moving average at 8.9636, indicating that short-term momentum remains tilted to the downside. Although the daily range has been extremely narrow between 8.8194 and 8.8400, the inability to reclaim the moving average suggests sellers continue to maintain control of the broader near-term trend.

The moving average configuration is one of the clearest signals available in the current dataset. Trading below the SMA(20) often reflects weakening market sentiment and reduced buying conviction. The MACD reading of -0.0208 is also negative, reinforcing the bearish bias and indicating that downward momentum has not fully dissipated. However, the lack of a signal-line value and unavailable RSI data limits confirmation, making price action around key levels particularly important to monitor.

From a technical perspective, immediate support appears near the session low at 8.8194. A decisive break below this area could expose additional downside toward the psychological 8.75 region, with potential extension toward 8.60 if selling accelerates. On the upside, initial resistance is located around 8.84, followed by the SMA(20) near 8.96. A sustained move above the moving average would improve the short-term outlook and could attract renewed buying interest toward the 9.10–9.20 zone.

For traders, a cautious approach remains appropriate while price trades beneath the SMA(20) and MACD stays negative. Conservative participants may prefer waiting for a confirmed breakout above 8.96 before considering bullish positions. More aggressive traders could monitor support around 8.82 for short-term rebound opportunities, provided risk is tightly controlled. Conversely, a breakdown below support may favor continuation-short strategies, targeting lower support zones while maintaining disciplined stop-loss placement above nearby resistance.
